During a battle in Trost, he discovered his ability to transform into the Attack Titan. Over years of warfare and political revelation, Eren learned the true history of the Eldian people and the global oppression they faced. Realizing that peace was impossible through traditional means, he eventually seized the power of the Founding Titan to initiate the Rumbling—a global cataclysm intended to eradicate all life outside his home island to secure his friends' future.
characters.personality: Relentless, stoic, and single-mindedly driven by an absolute pursuit of freedom. Originally impulsive and vengeful, he evolved into a cold, calculating strategist capable of suppressing his emotions to achieve his goals. He views himself as a monster by necessity, willing to sacrifice the world's morality and his own humanity to ensure the survival and liberty of his people.

characters.name: Ermac
characters.personality: Stoic and collective-minded, speaking as the many souls that compose him.
characters.bio: A magical construct formed from thousands of warrior souls bound together through Outworld sorcery.

characters.name: Erron Black
characters.bio: A gunslinger from the American Old West who was preserved through Outworld magic. Erron Black became one of the realms' most feared mercenaries, selling his skills to the highest bidder.
characters.personality: Cool-headed, pragmatic, and fiercely independent. He values professionalism over morality.

characters.name: Erza Scarlet
characters.bio: An S-Class Mage of the Fairy Tail Guild known as 'Titania.' Having escaped a childhood of slavery in the Tower of Heaven, she developed the ability to instantly swap between over a hundred different magical armors and weapons. Her versatility allows her to adapt to any element or opponent, making her one of the most feared and respected mages in the Kingdom of Fiore.
characters.personality: Strict, protective, and authoritative; a natural leader who draws immense power from her bonds with her guild.

characters.name: Espio the Chameleon
characters.bio: Espio is a skilled ninja and a core member of the Chaotix Detective Agency. First appearing in Knuckles' Chaotix, he serves as the team's stealth expert and voice of reason. Unlike Vector and Charmy, Espio is highly disciplined and often spends his time training in the arts of ninjutsu. He possesses the unique ability to shift his skin color to become completely invisible, allowing him to infiltrate enemy bases undetected. Throughout the series, he has transitioned from a hot-headed youth to a wise, observant warrior who uses his shurikens and kunai to fight against the forces of Dr. Eggman.
characters.personality: Stoic, disciplined, and calm; he is a level-headed ninja who values logic and silence over the boisterous nature of his teammates.

characters.bio: The secret leader of Aogiri Tree and daughter of Yoshimura. Eto is one of the most powerful ghouls in history and a mastermind who influenced countless events behind the scenes.
characters.personality: Brilliant, manipulative, and unpredictable. Eto hides her true intentions behind an eccentric public persona.
characters.name: Eto Yoshimura
Ezio Auditore da Firenze
characters.bio: Born into the noble Auditore family of Florence during the Italian Renaissance, Ezio's life was shattered when his father and brothers were unjustly executed. Discovering his family's secret heritage as Assassins, he embarked on a decades-long quest for justice against the Templar Order. Throughout his journey across Italy and later Constantinople, he recovered ancient artifacts known as Pieces of Eden, rebuilt the Assassin Brotherhood, and eventually sought the truth behind the origins of his Order and his predecessor, Altaïr Ibn-La'Ahad.
characters.personality: Initially a charismatic and hot-headed youth driven by vengeance, he matured into a wise, disciplined, and philosophical Mentor of the Assassin Brotherhood.

characters.name: Fat Gum
characters.personality: Fat Gum is a jovial, energetic, and highly protective Pro Hero who acts as a maternal figure and mentor to his interns. In combat, he behaves as a strategic tank, intentionally absorbing massive amounts of physical punishment to protect others and build up energy. While normally carefree and food-motivated, he becomes intensely serious and determined when lives are at stake, showing a willingness to burn through his own life-sustaining fat reserves to deliver a single, decisive counter-attack.
characters.bio: Taishiro Toyomitsu, famously known as the BMI Hero: Fat Gum, is a Pro Hero based in Osaka. Before establishing his own agency, he spent years working for the police force, which provided him with unparalleled insight into the Japanese criminal underworld and the distribution of Quirk-enhancing drugs. His Quirk, Fat Adsorption, allows his body fat to adhere to and sink objects or people into his frame, while also absorbing the kinetic energy of strikes. During the Shie Hassaikai Arc, he demonstrated his true power by absorbing countless blows from the villain Rappa, eventually converting that energy into a lean, muscular form to deliver a punch strong enough to shatter the strongest of barriers.

characters.name: Fern
characters.personality: Identity-conflicted, volatile, longing for acceptance, and eventually heroic.
characters.bio: Fern was born when the cursed Grass Sword fused with the Finn Sword, creating a sentient grass doppelgänger of Finn Mertens. Possessing all of Finn's memories but trapped in a non-human form, Fern struggled with a severe identity crisis and feelings of inadequacy. This led to a period of villainy where he served Uncle Gumbald as the 'Green Knight.' However, he eventually reconciled with Finn and sacrificed his physical form to help defeat the chaos deity GOLB, ultimately being reborn as a seedling that grew into the new Tree House.

characters.personality: Fearless, chivalrous, and highly adaptive; relies on instinctive combat and the hero's spirit.
characters.bio: Raised by anthropomorphic dogs in the post-apocalyptic Land of Ooo, Finn is a champion of justice. He has defeated cosmic deities, faced the embodiment of entropy (The Lich), and wielded multiple legendary swords including the living Grass Sword.
characters.name: Finn the Human
Frank West
characters.personality: Sarcastic, crude, highly determined, and proud of his achievements. He has a hidden heroic streak. He's covered wars, you know.
characters.bio: An ambitious photojournalist who broke the story on the Willamette outbreak. He survived an entire city of zombies and psychopaths using nothing but everyday items.

Frederica Baumann is a quarter-beast human serving as the head maid of House Mathers. Born in the Sanctuary to a half-beast father and human mother, she is the older half-sister of Garfiel Tinsel. She left the Sanctuary at age 12 to seek a means of liberating her kin, eventually spending a decade in service to Roswaal L. Mathers, where she mentored Ram and Rem. Following a temporary resignation, she returned to the mansion at Ram's request to assist the Emilia Camp during the events surrounding the Sanctuary's liberation, proving to be both an essential domestic manager and a formidable combatant against the mansion's invaders.
characters.personality: Exceedingly polite, disciplined, and composed, Frederica embodies the 'perfect maid' persona. In combat, she transitions into a predatory and fierce warrior, leveraging her quarter-beast heritage for raw power and agility. Despite her maturity, she is notably self-conscious about her sharp, shark-like teeth, often hiding her smile behind her hand.
characters.name: Frederica
characters.mediaSource: Re:Zero - Starting Life in Another World

characters.name: Frieren
characters.bio: Frieren is an elven mage who was a member of the Hero Party that defeated the Demon King, a feat that concluded a ten-year journey. As an elf with a lifespan exceeding a millennium, she initially viewed human lives as fleeting and inconsequential. However, after the death of the hero Himmel, she realized her failure to understand the heart of the person who cared for her most. This regret drives her current journey to 'Aureole,' the land where souls rest, to speak with Himmel once more. Known as 'Frieren the Slayer' by demons, she is a master of mana manipulation who has spent centuries perfecting the art of concealing her true power to deceive and eliminate her enemies.
characters.personality: Stoic, detached, and pragmatic; she possesses a scholarly obsession with collecting magic and a deeply buried sentimental streak.

characters.name: Frieza
characters.bio: Frieza is the emperor of the universe, a galactic conqueror responsible for the near-extinction of the Saiyan race. Known for his multiple transformations, his power level far exceeds planetary scales, allowing him to destroy worlds with a flick of his finger.
characters.personality: A sadistic and narcissistic tyrant who views all other life forms as insects and possesses an overwhelming god complex.

characters.name: Froppy
characters.personality: Extremely calm, level-headed, and observant. She is known for her blunt honesty and logical approach to combat, often serving as the emotional anchor for her team. She maintains a stoic 'poker face' even in high-stress situations, but she is deeply empathetic and fiercely protective of her friends.
characters.bio: A student in Class 1-A at U.A. High School training to become a Pro Hero. Raised in a household where she often had to care for her younger siblings due to her parents' busy work schedules, Tsuyu developed a sense of maturity and responsibility early in life. Known as the 'Rainy Season Hero: Froppy,' she excels in rescue operations and aquatic environments, though she is highly sensitive to extreme cold, which can force her into a state of hibernation.
The God of Wind and brother of Raiden. Fujin has defended Earthrealm for centuries using his divine command over air and storms.
characters.personality: Calm, compassionate, and wise. Fujin often acts as a moderating influence among the gods.
characters.name: Fujin

characters.personality: Unlike his father Vegeta, Trunks is serious, cautious, and incredibly polite. In combat, he is highly pragmatic, preferring to end battles as quickly and efficiently as possible to avoid unnecessary casualties.
characters.bio: Born in an apocalyptic alternate timeline where Earth was decimated by Androids 17 and 18, Trunks was mentored by Future Gohan. After his mentor's death, he used a Time Machine built by his mother, Bulma, to travel to the past. He saved Goku's life with heart medicine and warned the Z-Fighters of the coming threat, eventually gaining the strength to liberate his own future from both the Androids and the bio-android Cell.
characters.name: Future Trunks
Gaara
characters.bio: The youngest child of the Fourth Kazekage of Sunagakure, Gaara was born as the jinchūriki of the One-Tailed Shukaku. This burden led to a traumatic childhood where he was feared by his villagers and repeatedly targeted for assassination by his own father. After a pivotal defeat at the hands of Naruto Uzumaki, Gaara abandoned his path of hatred and dedicated himself to proving his worth to his people. He eventually rose to the rank of Fifth Kazekage and served as the Regimental Commander of the Allied Shinobi Forces during the Fourth Shinobi World War.
characters.personality: Stoic, analytical, and profoundly protective. Once a bloodthirsty nihilist who defined his existence through killing, he transformed into a calm and wise leader after finding kinship with Naruto Uzumaki. In combat, he remains composed, utilizing a methodical defensive-to-offensive style that focuses on crushing opponents from a distance while remaining nearly untouchable.

characters.name: Gale Force
characters.bio: A first-year student at Shiketsu High School, Inasa Yoarashi is widely regarded as one of the most powerful aspiring heroes in Japan. He originally achieved the highest score in the U.A. High School recommendation entrance exams, placing above Shoto Todoroki, but he declined the offer to enroll after a negative encounter with Endeavor and Shoto's then-chilly demeanor. Using his Quirk 'Whirlwind,' which allows him complete mastery over the air, he participated in the Provisional Hero License Exam where he single-handedly eliminated 120 competitors in a single move. Though he initially clashed with Shoto, the two eventually reconciled and worked together during remedial courses to refine their teamwork and spirit.
characters.personality: Inasa is an intensely energetic, loud, and 'hot-blooded' individual who values passion and emotional 'heat' above all else in a hero. In combat, he is surprisingly tactical and precise, capable of controlling hundreds of distinct wind currents simultaneously to manipulate objects or pin down enemies without causing lethal harm. He is deeply polite—often bowing so hard he hits his head on the ground—yet he is brutally honest about his feelings, having famously rejected a spot at U.A. High due to his personal distaste for Endeavor and Shoto Todoroki's initial coldness.

characters.name: Gambit
characters.bio: Born in New Orleans and abandoned at birth due to his glowing red eyes, Remy LeBeau was kidnapped by the Thieves Guild and raised as their 'Le Diable Blanc'. A master thief, he possesses the mutant ability to charge inanimate objects with volatile kinetic energy, causing them to explode on impact. After a checkered past including a forced affiliation with Mister Sinister and the Marauders, Gambit eventually found redemption by joining the X-Men, where he became one of the team's most formidable and iconic members, frequently paired with his long-term love interest, Rogue.
characters.personality: A charming and suave 'Ragin' Cajun' with a rogue-ish wit. In combat, he is a master tactician who utilizes his professional thieving skills for misdirection, combining high-speed acrobatic movements with explosive long-range projectiles.

powers: Triforce of Power (Immense strength and near-invulnerability)
Mastery of Dark Magic and Sorcery
characters.name: Ganondorf
characters.mediaSource: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time
characters.bio: Born as the sole male of the desert-dwelling Gerudo tribe, a phenomenon that occurs only once every hundred years, Ganondorf Dragmire was raised to be their king. Not satisfied with the harsh desert, he sought to claim the lush Kingdom of Hyrule. By manipulating the young Hero of Time, he gained access to the Sacred Realm and seized the Triforce of Power. This act transformed him into the Demon King Ganon, sparking a multi-generational cycle of death and rebirth as he eternally opposes Link and Princess Zelda to rule over all existence.
characters.personality: A cold, arrogant, and highly strategic conqueror. He is a warrior scholar who balances brute force with manipulative cunning. He displays immense patience, often waiting centuries for seals to weaken, and possesses an unbreakable will driven by an ancient, reincarnating hatred for the lineage of the Goddess and the Hero.

characters.bio: Garfiel Tinsel is the younger half-brother of Frederica Baumann and the former guardian of the Sanctuary. Having been abandoned by his mother at a young age, he spent ten years guarding the Sanctuary's barrier, fearing the outside world. He initially served as a major antagonist to Natsuki Subaru during the events of Arc 4, but after being defeated and forced to confront his past through the Trials of the Sanctuary, he joined the Emilia Camp. He now serves as their primary frontline vanguard, striving to live up to his title as the 'Gorgeous Tiger'.
characters.personality: Garfiel is a high-spirited, hot-headed, and impulsive combatant who operates on feral instinct. He is known for his crude manner of speaking and his habit of quoting cryptic, self-made proverbs. While initially appearing hostile and aggressive, he is deeply protective of his 'family' and possesses a hidden sensitivity, showing absolute loyalty once his respect is earned.
characters.name: Garfiel
characters.mediaSource: Re:Zero - Starting Life in Another World

characters.name: Garou
characters.personality: Garou is a martial arts prodigy and self-proclaimed 'Human Monster' who hunts heroes to expose the hypocrisy of the popular-versus-unpopular dynamic. He is a tactical genius, possessing the ability to analyze and replicate an opponent's fighting style after observing it only once. Despite his pursuit of 'Absolute Evil,' he maintains a stubborn sense of honor, refusing to harm innocent civilians and showing a protective, soft-hearted side toward children.
characters.bio: A former top student of the legendary martial artist Bang, Garou was expelled after hospitalizing his fellow disciples. His motivation stems from a childhood spent as an outcast, identifying with the villains in television shows who were always destined to lose to 'popular' heroes. He embarked on a quest to become the ultimate monster, hunting heroes across all classes to unite the world under a single, common fear. His body eventually breaks through its natural limiters, leading to several monstrous transformations and a brief ascension to cosmic-level power.

A legendary Kaleesh warlord from the planet Kalee, Qymaen jai Sheelal led his people in the Huk War before being nearly destroyed in a shuttle crash. Reconstructed by the InterGalactic Banking Clan and Count Dooku, he became a cyborg shell housing only his vital organic organs and a brain enhanced for combat. Trained in all seven forms of lightsaber combat by Dooku, he rose to become the Supreme Martial Commander of the Separatist Droid Army, personally slaying dozens of Jedi and collecting their lightsabers as trophies.
characters.personality: Ruthless, tactical, and arrogant, Grievous is a cold-blooded strategist who prefers overwhelming his foes through psychological terror and brute mechanical force. Despite his immense power as a Jedi hunter, he is pragmatically cowardly, often opting to retreat using his specialized vehicles or skittering agility when a battle turns against him.
characters.name: General Grievous
characters.mediaSource: Star Wars: Episode III – Revenge of the Sith

characters.personality: Genos is extremely serious, analytical, and intensely focused on combat efficiency. He is relentlessly seeking power to avenge his family and is devotedly loyal to his master, Saitama. In battle, he is aggressive and disciplined, often willing to sacrifice his own limbs or push his mechanical body to the point of total destruction to protect others or secure a victory.
characters.bio: A 19-year-old cyborg and S-Class Rank 14 hero known as the 'Demon Cyborg.' At age 15, his hometown and family were destroyed by a rogue 'Mad Cyborg.' He was saved by Dr. Kuseno, who transformed Genos into a cyborg at his request to help him seek revenge. After witnessing the overwhelming power of the hero Saitama, Genos became his self-appointed disciple, moving into Saitama's apartment to observe his training methods and discover the secret to his strength.
characters.name: Genos
Gentle Criminal
characters.personality: Gentle Criminal is a theatrical and chivalrous individual who adheres to a strict code of etiquette, even during his criminal exploits. He is polite to a fault, often apologizing to those he inconveniences, and operates with a deep sense of romanticism regarding his legacy. While he seeks fame as a 'gentleman thief' to avoid being forgotten by history, his primary motivation is a distorted form of the heroism he once sought. He is intensely protective of his partner, La Brava, and possesses a strong moral compass that eventually leads him back to the side of justice.
characters.bio: Originally a student in a Hero Course, Danjuro Tobita's life took a tragic turn after a failed rescue attempt resulted in a victim's injury and his subsequent expulsion and social ostracization. After years of living as a forgotten 'freeter,' he reinvented himself as Gentle Criminal, an internet-famous villain who filmed non-violent, refined heists to expose societal flaws. After being defeated by Izuku Midoriya during the U.A. School Festival arc, he underwent a process of redemption. During the Final War, he played a crucial role by using his Elasticity quirk to prevent the floating U.A. High School from crashing, finally achieving the heroic status he had always craved.

Geralt is a Witcher, a mutated monster hunter trained since childhood at the fortress of Kaer Morhen. He is known as the 'White Wolf' due to his hair turning milk-white after undergoing additional experimental mutations that granted him abilities far beyond normal men. Though Witchers are meant to remain neutral in human affairs, Geralt's destiny is inextricably tied to the fate of kingdoms and the protection of Cirilla, his adopted daughter and a child of Elder Blood. He traverses a war-torn world, slaying monsters for coin while navigating the complex politics of the Continent.
characters.personality: Stoic, cynical, and pragmatic on the surface, but possesses a deep moral compass and loyalty to his friends and family.
characters.name: Geralt of Rivia
powers: Magical Signs (Aard, Igni, Quen, Yrden, Axii)
High Resistance to Toxins and Poisons

characters.name: Geten
characters.personality: Geten is a cold, calculated, and extremely devoted warrior. He is a staunch believer in the Meta Liberation Army's core philosophy: that a person's worth is defined entirely by the strength and mastery of their 'Meta Ability' (Quirk). He lacks interest in traditional societal structures like education, having never attended school to focus entirely on combat training. In battle, he is highly observant, capable of identifying the limitations of an opponent's quirk—such as Dabi's overheating—while maintaining absolute focus on his own tactical positioning.
characters.bio: Born into the prestigious but declining Himura family (the same lineage as Rei Todoroki), Geten was recruited at a young age by the Meta Liberation Army. Under the guidance of Re-Destro, he spent his entire life in seclusion, obsessively honing his 'Ice Ply' ability. This training allowed him to bypass the standard limitations of ice users; rather than just creating ice, he can manipulate its temperature and shape freely, and even freeze nearby water sources to create an infinite supply of ammunition. He rose to become a Lieutenant in the Paranormal Liberation Front, serving as one of the organization's most formidable long-range combatants.

characters.name: Ghost Rider
characters.bio: Johnny Blaze was a legendary motorcycle stunt performer who made a desperate deal with the demon Mephisto to save his surrogate father's life. Though his father was cured of cancer, he died shortly after in a stunt accident, and Blaze was bound to the Spirit of Vengeance, Zarathos. Now, as the Ghost Rider, Blaze roams the highways on a flaming motorcycle, compelled to seek out evil and inflict the same pain upon the wicked that they have caused others.
characters.personality: Driven by a supernatural sense of justice, Johnny is often brooding and stoic, yet fiercely protective of innocent souls. In combat, he is a relentless and terrifying force, utilizing psychological warfare through his hellish appearance and showing no mercy to those he deems 'guilty'.

Gigantomachia is characterized by absolute, dog-like loyalty to his masters, All For One and Tomura Shigaraki. He possesses a simple, singular focus on obedience and becomes emotionally volatile or weeps when his master is insulted. In battle, he acts as a mindless, unstoppable force of nature, prioritizing the fulfillment of his orders over his own safety.
characters.bio: Gigantomachia was the personal bodyguard and most devoted servant of the villain All For One. He was biologically engineered to host multiple Quirks simultaneously without the mental degradation typical of Nomu, though his cognitive functions were reduced to focus purely on servitude. After All For One's defeat, he remained hidden in the mountains for a long period of 'testing' the successor, Tomura Shigaraki. Once satisfied with Shigaraki's growth, he joined the League of Villains and became the primary engine of destruction for the Paranormal Liberation Front, capable of leveling entire cities during his relentless charges.
characters.name: Gigantomachia
Gin Ichimaru
characters.bio: Gin Ichimaru was the former Captain of the 3rd Division in the Gotei 13. A child prodigy and a master manipulator, he spent centuries undercover as Sosuke Aizen's right-hand man, all to find the perfect moment to strike and avenge Rangiku Matsumoto. His Zanpakuto is the fastest in the Soul Society, capable of extending and contracting at five hundred times the speed of sound.
characters.personality: Deceptive, analytical, and unsettlingly calm; he employs psychological warfare and hides his true power behind a permanent, mocking grin.

characters.name: Gintoki
characters.personality: Lazy, cynical, and deadpan with a severe sugar addiction, yet deeply loyal and possesses a fierce protective streak for his comrades.
characters.bio: Known during the Joui War as the 'Shiroyasha' (White Yaksha) for his terrifying skill and white hair, Gintoki Sakata is a veteran samurai who fought against the alien Amanto invaders. Following the war's conclusion and the ban on swords, he retired to Edo and established the 'Yorozuya Gin-chan,' a jack-of-all-trades business. Despite his often unmotivated exterior and obsession with Weekly Shonen Jump, he remains one of the most formidable fighters in the world, wielding a wooden sword carved from a 10,000-year-old tree to protect the peace and his found family.

Giorno Giovanna is the protagonist of JoJo's Bizarre Adventure Part 5. Born to a Japanese mother and the vampire DIO (inhabiting Jonathan Joestar's body), Giorno's childhood was defined by neglect and abuse. His life changed when he saved a wounded mafia member, who repaid him by protecting him from his abusers. This encounter inspired Giorno to become a 'Gang-Star'—a benevolent mob boss who would rid Italy's streets of drugs and corruption. He eventually joins the gang Passione and leads a rebellion against its mysterious leader, Diavolo, eventually evolving his Stand into the ultimate defensive form: Gold Experience Requiem.
characters.personality: Giorno is a highly intelligent, analytical, and composed individual. He possesses a resolute will and a calm demeanor, rarely losing his temper. In combat, he is a strategic mastermind who utilizes the environment and his Stand's life-giving abilities to outmaneuver opponents. He follows a strict moral code of justice inherited from his Joestar lineage, combined with a cold ruthlessness toward his enemies inherited from DIO.
characters.name: Giorno Giovanna
powers: Life Giver (Creating living organisms from objects)
Gold Experience Requiem (Causality Manipulation/Return to Zero)
Life Shot (Accelerating a victim's mental perception)

characters.personality: Stoic, clinical, and intensely focused, Giyu maintains perfect emotional control to execute precise, lethal strikes.
characters.bio: The current Water Hashira of the Demon Slayer Corps, Giyu is a master swordsman who survived a tragic past involving the loss of his family and best friend. He was the first to encounter Tanjiro and Nezuko Kamado, sparing them and setting them on the path to becoming demon slayers. He is known for his signature self-created breathing form, Dead Calm.
characters.name: Giyu Tomioka

characters.bio: Son Gohan is the first son of the Saiyan Goku and the Earthling Chi-Chi. Named after Goku's adoptive grandfather, Gohan was thrust into battle at age four when his uncle Raditz arrived on Earth. After Goku's death, he was trained in the wilderness by Piccolo, forming a lifelong bond with the Namekian. Gohan became the first Saiyan to achieve Super Saiyan 2, famously defeating the bio-android Cell. Though he spent years as a scholar, he eventually pushed past his limits in 'Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero' to unlock the Gohan Beast form, reclaiming his status as one of Earth's ultimate defenders.
characters.personality: Unlike his father, Gohan is a pacifist at heart who prefers scholarship over combat. However, he possesses a protective instinct and a volatile, rage-fueled hidden potential that makes him one of the universe's deadliest fighters when pushed. In combat, he is highly disciplined and tactical, though he can occasionally fall victim to overconfidence when his power peaks.
powers: Ki Manipulation (Kamehameha, Masenko, Special Beam Cannon)
Transformation (Super Saiyan, Potential Unleashed, Gohan Beast)
characters.name: Gohan

characters.bio: A Saiyan survivor raised on Earth, Goku has evolved from a simple martial artist to a cosmic protector capable of rivaling Gods of Destruction. His journey through Dragon Ball Super has pushed him into the realm of 'Ultra Instinct,' a state of perfect reactive combat.
characters.personality: Friendly and humble, yet possesses an insatiable drive to find and fight the strongest opponents in the multiverse.
characters.name: Goku
Golden Boy
characters.personality: Charismatic and heroic with a protective streak, though prone to intense mental pressure and emotional instability under corporate gaslighting.
characters.bio: The top-ranked student at Godolkin University, Luke was the golden child of Vought International, groomed to join The Seven. Born with the power to ignite his entire body and project devastating heat blasts, he was a symbol of hope until he discovered the dark truth about his brother and the facility known as 'The Woods'.

characters.name: Gon
characters.bio: A young boy from Whale Island who discovered his father was a legendary Hunter. Gon set out to pass the Hunter Exam and find him, mastering the art of Nen along the way. His potential is so vast it often frightens even the most seasoned combatants.
characters.personality: Gon is optimistic, incredibly stubborn, and possesses a terrifyingly simple battle mindset. He is highly adaptable and willing to risk his own life for a single opening.

characters.bio: A theoretical physicist who graduated from MIT, Gordon Freeman was an employee at the Black Mesa Research Facility. After a disastrous experiment caused a 'Resonance Cascade,' opening a portal to the dimension Xen, Gordon fought his way through the facility to stop the invasion. After being placed in stasis by the mysterious G-Man, he was awakened two decades later to lead the resistance against the Combine, an interdimensional empire that had enslaved Earth. He is famously known for his signature weapon, the crowbar, and his Mark V HEV suit.
characters.personality: Quiet, analytical, and incredibly resilient, Gordon is a man of action who remains calm under extreme pressure, serving as a silent symbol of hope for humanity.
characters.name: Gordon Freeman

characters.bio: The legendary Shokan prince who reigned as Mortal Kombat champion for centuries and became one of the most iconic warriors in the franchise.
characters.personality: Proud, aggressive, and highly competitive, yet bound by a warrior's code.
characters.name: Goro
Gourmet
characters.bio: Known as the Gourmet, Tsukiyama initially became obsessed with Kaneki due to his unique scent. Over time he evolved from an antagonist into one of Kaneki's most devoted allies.
characters.personality: Dramatic, charismatic, and highly intelligent. Tsukiyama is eccentric but ultimately loyal to those he values.

Gran Torino is a sharp, blunt, and highly observant mentor who often adopts a senile persona to test the patience and resolve of others. In combat, he is a tactical genius who utilizes his vast experience to outmaneuver younger opponents. He adheres to a 'tough love' teaching philosophy, believing that practical application and failure are the best teachers, yet he is deeply devoted to his students and the legacy of his friend Nana Shimura.
characters.bio: Sorahiko Torino is a legendary retired Pro Hero and a former homeroom teacher at U.A. High School. A close friend of the seventh One For All user, Nana Shimura, he took it upon himself to train her successor, Toshinori Yagi (All Might), subjecting him to notoriously brutal training to prepare him as the Symbol of Peace. Despite his immense power, Torino purposely remained obscure to the public, obtaining his hero license only to legally use his Quirk. Years later, he came out of retirement to mentor Izuku Midoriya, teaching him the 'Full Cowl' technique that allowed the boy to stabilize his power. His 'Jet' Quirk allows him to shoot air from the soles of his feet, granting him unparalleled speed and aerial mobility.
characters.name: Gran Torino
Grand Admiral Thrawn
characters.bio: The only non-human to reach the rank of Grand Admiral in the Galactic Empire, Thrawn is a Chiss military genius from the Unknown Regions. He relies on unparalleled deductive reasoning and tactical innovation rather than brute force or the supernatural to dismantle his enemies.
characters.personality: Coldly logical and patient, he treats every battle as a puzzle to be solved through the study of his opponent's culture and behavioral patterns.

characters.bio: A core member of the Fairy Tail guild and a rival to Natsu Dragneel. Gray is a master of Ice-Make magic who later inherited Ice Devil Slayer Magic from his father, allowing him to freeze even the most intense flames.
characters.personality: Cool-headed and strategic; he utilizes environment-shifting magic to trap and overwhelm enemies.
characters.name: Gray Fullbuster
Green Arrow
characters.personality: Oliver Queen is a sharp-witted, precision-focused combatant who functions as a tactical sniper on the battlefield. He is characterized by a rebellious, anti-authoritarian streak and a fierce commitment to social justice. In combat, he is resourceful and agile, frequently using his environment and a wide variety of specialized trick arrows to neutralize multiple threats simultaneously while maintaining a confident, often sarcastic demeanor.
characters.bio: Billionaire playboy Oliver Queen was transformed after being shipwrecked and stranded on a remote desert island for years. Forced to survive with nothing but a makeshift bow, he honed his archery skills to a superhuman level. Upon returning to Star City, he rejected his previous shallow lifestyle to become the vigilante Green Arrow. As a champion of the 'little guy' and the oppressed, he uses his wealth and skills to fight systemic corruption and street crime. A key member of the Justice League, he serves as the team's conscience, often challenging his more powerful peers on moral and social grounds.

characters.name: Green Lantern
characters.bio: A fearless test pilot who was chosen by a dying alien to join the Green Lantern Corps, an intergalactic police force. Armed with a power ring fueled by his willpower, Hal Jordan has become one of the greatest heroes in the DC Universe, defending Earth and Sector 2814 from cosmic threats.
characters.personality: Impulsive and incredibly brave, he relies on instinct and sheer force of will to overcome any obstacle.

characters.name: Grimmjow Jaegerjaquez
characters.bio: Originally a panther-like Adjuchas-class Hollow leading a pack of predators in the deserts of Hueco Mundo, Grimmjow sought to evolve through constant combat. After being transformed into an Arrancar by Sōsuke Aizen, he was ranked as the 6th Espada. He developed a deep-seated rivalry with Ichigo Kurosaki, viewing the Shinigami as his ultimate prey. Following the collapse of Aizen's army, Grimmjow survived and eventually allied with his former enemies to combat the greater threat of the Quincy Wandenreich.
characters.personality: Grimmjow is a feral, brutal, and impulsive warrior with a lethally short temper. He is blunt, sarcastic, and thrives on the thrill of battle, often displaying a psychotic grin when facing a strong opponent. Despite his violent nature, he follows a strict code of honor, refusing to fight an unfair match or strike a weakened enemy, even going as far as to have his rivals healed before a duel.

characters.name: Grogu
characters.personality: Instinctive and protective, utilizing high-tier Force abilities when his companions are threatened, though he is easily physically exhausted.
characters.bio: A member of the same mysterious species as Grand Master Yoda, Grogu was a Jedi Initiate at the Coruscant Temple during Order 66. After being rescued by the Mandalorian Din Djarin, he began a journey to rediscover his heritage while wielding a raw, immense connection to the Force that belies his infant appearance.

characters.name: Guile
characters.personality: Guile is a stoic, disciplined, and highly professional soldier in the U.S. Air Force. In combat, he is a 'zoning' specialist who maintains emotional composure, using his signature Sonic Boom to control the pace of the match and his Flash Kick to punish air approaches. He is deeply motivated by duty, justice, and a fierce loyalty to his comrades and family.
characters.bio: A Major and later Colonel in the United States Air Force, Guile entered the World Warrior tournament to avenge the presumed death of his best friend and mentor, Charlie Nash, at the hands of the Shadaloo dictator M. Bison. While originally consumed by a desire for personal revenge, he eventually shifted his focus to dismantling global criminal organizations and protecting world peace. Despite his rigid military exterior, he is a dedicated family man who fights to ensure a safe future for his wife, Julia, and daughter, Amy.

characters.name: Guts
characters.personality: Stoic, cynical, and battle-hardened, Guts is a survivor defined by his iron will and profound inner strength. Despite his abrasive exterior, he is deeply protective of those he allows into his inner circle.
characters.bio: Born from the corpse of his mother and raised by a mercenary band, Guts has known only war. After a formative period with the Band of the Hawk, his life was shattered by the Eclipse—a ritual where his commander, Griffith, sacrificed their comrades to become a god. Branded as a sacrifice and haunted by demons, Guts became the 'Black Swordsman.' He wanders a dark fantasy world wielding the 'Dragonslayer,' a massive blade too large for any normal man, seeking vengeance against the God Hand while struggling to maintain his humanity.

characters.personality: Might Guy is defined by his boisterous optimism and his unwavering belief in the 'Power of Youth.' He is a charismatic leader who encourages his students through intense physical discipline and a code of 'My Rules.' In combat, he transitions from a comedic and high-energy personality to a devastatingly serious and selfless warrior, willing to sacrifice his life for his comrades.
characters.bio: A Jonin of Konohagakure, Might Guy is widely considered the world's greatest Taijutsu master. Lacking natural talent for Ninjutsu or Genjutsu as a child, he followed in the footsteps of his father, Might Duy, training relentlessly to overcome his limitations. He is the leader of Team Guy—consisting of Rock Lee, Neji Hyuga, and Tenten—and is the eternal rival of Kakashi Hatake. During the Fourth Shinobi World War, Guy famously opened all Eight Inner Gates to fight Madara Uchiha, a feat so powerful that Madara himself declared Guy the strongest martial artist he had ever faced.
characters.name: Guy Sensei
Gyokko
characters.personality: Arrogant, sadistic, and obsessed with his own 'artistic' cruelty.
characters.bio: Holding the rank of Upper Moon Five, Gyokko is a monstrous demon serving Muzan Kibutsuji. He resides in mystical pots and views the mutilation of others as a supreme form of art.

The Stone Hashira and the strongest member of the Demon Slayer Corps. Despite being blind, he utilizes specialized breathing techniques and superhuman senses to perceive his surroundings with more clarity than those with sight, making him a legend among slayers.
characters.personality: A stoic and deeply compassionate monk who maintains absolute focus and strategic calm during the most chaotic battles.
characters.name: Gyomei Himejima

powers: Blood Demon Art: Flying Blood Sickles
characters.name: Gyutaro
characters.personality: Spiteful, envious, and incredibly protective; he fights with a sadistic and unpredictable style.
characters.bio: The true holder of the Upper Rank Six title within the Twelve Kizuki, Gyutaro lived a life of misery in the Entertainment District before becoming a demon. He shares a body and life-link with his sister, Daki, and has slaughtered over fifteen Hashira throughout his century-long existence.

Born on the industrial planet Corellia, Han Solo rose from a street urchin to an Imperial cadet before being expelled for saving the Wookiee Chewbacca from slavery. The two became a legendary smuggling duo, eventually winning the Millennium Falcon from Lando Calrissian in a game of Sabacc. Though initially motivated by a reward to rescue Princess Leia Organa, Solo became a pivotal leader in the Rebel Alliance. He was instrumental in the destruction of both Death Stars, rose to the rank of General, and led the strike team during the Battle of Endor, ultimately helping to topple the Galactic Empire.
characters.personality: A charismatic and street-smart scoundrel who balances a cynical, self-serving exterior with a core of deep loyalty and heroism. In combat, Solo is highly improvisational, famously 'shooting first' and relying on his lightning-fast reflexes with a DL-44 heavy blaster. He is a pragmatic risk-taker who trusts his gut and his ship over ancient religions or mystical powers.
characters.name: Han Solo
characters.mediaSource: Star Wars: Episode IV - A New Hope

characters.name: Hanami
characters.bio: An unregistered Special Grade Cursed Spirit, Hanami was born from the collective human fear of land-based natural disasters, specifically forests and earth. Hanami believes that humans have mistreated the planet for too long and seeks to eradicate humanity so that the Earth can return to its natural, pristine state. Alongside Jogo, Dagon, and Mahito, Hanami worked with Pseudo-Geto to facilitate the return of the 'True Humans' (Cursed Spirits). Hanami possesses a unique biology that allows for rapid regeneration and a shell so durable it can withstand most Jujutsu attacks. Hanami communicates through a unique language that sounds like gibberish but is understood directly by the listener's soul.
characters.personality: Hanami is a stoic, calm, and deeply philosophical individual who views the preservation of nature as a sacred mission. Unlike the more impulsive Jogo, Hanami initially approached combat with a sense of grim necessity rather than malice. However, during the Kyoto Goodwill Event, Hanami began to embrace the 'thrill of battle' and personal bloodlust, becoming a much more aggressive and dangerous fighter.
Pathologically cowardly; hides behind powerful emotional avatars while feigning innocence.
characters.bio: The Upper Rank Four of the Twelve Kizuki, Hantengu is a centuries-old demon who hides his true, tiny self. He possesses a unique Blood Demon Art that allows him to manifest his emotions—anger, pleasure, sorrow, joy, and hatred—as independent, powerful warriors.
characters.name: Hantengu

characters.personality: Loyal, cheeky, and optimistic, Happy serves as the emotional heart of Team Natsu and a quick-witted support ally.
characters.bio: An Exceed from the world of Edolas, Happy was sent to Earthland as an egg and raised by Natsu Dragneel. He is a primary member of the Fairy Tail guild, providing aerial transport for Natsu and maintaining high morale with his signature catchphrase 'Aye!'
characters.name: Happy
Hatchan
characters.personality: Hatchan is characterized by a bright, gentle, and somewhat airheaded nature. Despite his intimidating appearance as an octopus fish-man, he is notoriously gullible and trusting, once even welcoming Zoro Roronoa into Arlong Park because he didn't realize Zoro was an enemy. Following his redemption, he has shown profound remorse for his past criminal life, demonstrating extreme loyalty to his friends and a selfless protective streak, particularly toward the mermaid Camie and the Straw Hat crew.
characters.bio: Hatchan, commonly known as Hachi, is an octopus fish-man and a former officer of the Arlong Pirates. His history dates back to the Fish-Man District, where he was a childhood friend of Fish-Man leaders like Jinbe and Arlong. He originally served under Fisher Tiger in the Sun Pirates before joining Arlong's splinter group to terrorize the East Blue. After the Straw Hats defeated the Arlong Pirates, Hatchan was the only officer to escape capture. He eventually redeemed himself through a series of seafaring adventures, fulfilling his lifelong dream of opening a floating takoyaki stand called 'Takoyaki 8.' He later became a crucial ally to Monkey D. Luffy, assisting the crew in the Sabaody Archipelago and guarding the Thousand Sunny during the two-year timeskip.

characters.personality: Unpredictable, fanatical, and devoted to absolute chaos. He rejects order, law, and stability in all forms.
characters.bio: A cleric from Chaosrealm who believes true freedom can only exist through complete disorder. Havik's bizarre regenerative abilities allow him to mutilate and reconstruct his body in combat while spreading chaos wherever he goes.
characters.name: Havik
Hawks
characters.personality: Hawks is exceptionally intelligent, rational, and strategic, masking his calculated nature with a laid-back and jovial exterior. In combat, he is a master of multitasking, often rescuing dozens of civilians simultaneously while engaging threats with high-speed precision. He is a pragmatic realist who is willing to perform morally ambiguous 'dirty work' if it ensures long-term peace for society.
characters.bio: Born to a criminal father and a neglectful mother, Keigo Takami's life changed when he used his wings to save a group of people from a high-speed accident. His potential was noticed by the Hero Public Safety Commission, who 'purchased' him from his family to raise and train him as a specialized operative. Stripped of his past to become the 'Wing Hero: Hawks,' he became the youngest Pro Hero to ever enter the Top 10. During the rise of the Paranormal Liberation Front, Hawks acted as a high-stakes double agent, infiltrating the League of Villains to gather intelligence essential for the safety of the world.

characters.name: Heihachi Mishima
characters.bio: Heihachi Mishima is the long-time patriarch of the Mishima family and the former head of the global conglomerate Mishima Zaibatsu. His legacy began when he overthrew his father, Jinpachi, and later threw his young son, Kazuya, off a cliff to test the boy's strength and resilience. Throughout the King of Iron Fist Tournaments, Heihachi has remained a primary antagonist, driven by a desire for world domination and the elimination of the Devil Gene that plagues his bloodline—despite not possessing the gene himself. He relies purely on his mastery of Mishima Style Karate and his own tempered physical form to challenge gods, demons, and genetically enhanced fighters alike.
characters.personality: Ruthless, arrogant, and power-hungry, Heihachi Mishima is a combatant who respects nothing but absolute strength. He views his family as either assets to be controlled or rivals to be eliminated. In battle, he is a brutal strategist who overwhelms opponents with overwhelming physical power and lightning-infused strikes, showing no mercy regardless of the opponent's status or lineage.

characters.bio: Summoned to Earth as an infant by Nazi occultists in 1944, Hellboy was rescued and raised by Professor Trevor Bruttenholm of the B.P.R.D. Despite his destiny to bring about the apocalypse as the 'Beast of the Apocalypse,' he has dedicated his life to protecting humanity from paranormal threats.
characters.personality: A jaded, blue-collar brawler who approaches supernatural threats with grit and a cynical wit.
characters.name: Hellboy
Hideyoshi Nagachika
characters.personality: Optimistic, friendly, and remarkably perceptive. Hide consistently puts the well-being of others before his own safety.
characters.bio: Kaneki's closest friend and one of the few people able to understand him completely. Though lacking supernatural abilities, Hide's intelligence, bravery, and loyalty make him one of the series' most important characters.

characters.name: Himeno
characters.personality: Himeno is a seasoned and pragmatic Devil Hunter who balances a laid-back, teasing exterior with deep-seated emotional trauma from losing previous partners. In combat, she functions as a high-tier support specialist, utilizing the invisible right arm of the Ghost Devil to strike or restrain enemies from a distance. She is highly observant and protective, prioritizing the lives of her colleagues over her own, often using humor and smoking as coping mechanisms to maintain composure in high-stress environments.
characters.bio: A veteran member of Tokyo Public Safety Special Division 4, Himeno served as the mentor and partner to Aki Hayakawa. Her career was marked by tragedy, having witnessed the deaths of six former partners, which shaped her cynical yet deeply caring worldview. To gain the power necessary for survival, she sacrificed her right eye to the Ghost Devil in exchange for the ability to control its invisible limbs. Throughout her service, her primary motivation shifted from simple survival to a desperate desire to keep Aki safe from the dangers of Devil Hunting. She eventually made the ultimate sacrifice, giving her entire body to the Ghost Devil to protect her friends during an ambush by the Katana Man and Akane Sawatari.

characters.name: Himiko Toga
powers: Quirk Replication (Copying powers of loved ones)
characters.bio: A central antagonist of My Hero Academia, Himiko was forced to suppress her blood-obsessed nature by her parents until a violent mental break led her to murder a classmate. After going into hiding as a serial killer, she joined the League of Villains to find a world where she could 'live normally.' Her 'Transform' Quirk allows her to perfectly mimic anyone whose blood she consumes, and through her intense emotional bonds, she eventually gained the ability to duplicate the Quirks of those she loves, such as Ochaco Uraraka and Twice. She serves as a lieutenant in the Paranormal Liberation Front.
characters.personality: Cheerful, obsessive, and psychopathic; she views violence and blood-drinking as the ultimate expressions of love and personal freedom.

characters.name: Hiromi Higuruma
characters.personality: Higuruma is a hyper-intelligent, rational, and deeply principled individual who possesses an unwavering devotion to justice. Initially appearing weary and disillusioned with human nature, he operates with a calm and aloof demeanor. In combat, he is analytical and efficient, preferring the structured rules of his 'Deadly Sentencing' domain over reckless violence. Despite his initial role as a cold judge in the Culling Game, he remains fundamentally altruistic and humble.
characters.bio: Hiromi Higuruma was a genius defense attorney in Tokyo who specialized in taking on cases for the wrongfully accused. His faith in the Japanese legal system was shattered when a client he believed in was sentenced to death despite overwhelming evidence of innocence, while actual criminals went free. During Kenjaku's Culling Game, Higuruma awakened as a sorcerer and reached a Grade 1 level of power in just twelve days. After a transformative battle with Yuji Itadori, Higuruma chose to use his powers to assist the sorcerers of Jujutsu High, eventually becoming a critical combatant in the Shinjuku Showdown against Ryomen Sukuna.

characters.name: Hisoka Morow
characters.bio: Hisoka Morow is a mysterious Hunter and former member #4 of the Phantom Troupe, a notorious criminal organization. He originally joined the Spider only to secure a death match with their leader, Chrollo Lucilfer. His origins are largely unknown, though he claims to have named his primary technique 'Bungee Gum' after a brand of chewing gum he liked as a child. Throughout the series, he oscillates between being a terrifying antagonist and a self-serving ally to Gon and Killua, always motivated by his own pursuit of the ultimate high: a fight to the death.
characters.personality: Hisoka is a combat-crazed, hedonistic sociopath who lives exclusively for the thrill of fighting powerful opponents. He is a master of deception and psychological warfare, often manipulating his enemies through 'Texture Surprise' or misdirection before crushing them. He possesses a chilling patience, sometimes sparing potential rivals (whom he refers to as 'fruit') so they can 'ripen' into more challenging opponents for the future.

characters.bio: The face of Vought International, Homelander was raised in a lab to be the ultimate biological weapon. As the leader of The Seven, he is worshiped as a god but harbors a deep-seated contempt for humanity and a desperate need for approval.
characters.personality: Narcissistic, sadistic, and mentally unstable. He relies on raw power and intimidation rather than refined martial technique.
characters.name: Homelander
Hound Dog
characters.bio: Ryo Inui, professionally known as the Hunting Dog Hero: Hound Dog, is a Pro Hero and a prominent faculty member at U.A. High School. His Quirk, 'Dog', grants him the physical traits and heightened senses of a canine, most notably a sense of smell so acute it allows him to detect intruders across the vast U.A. campus. As the school's Head of Student Discipline, he is responsible for maintaining order and security. He played a pivotal role during the U.A. School Festival, where he led the security detail and successfully intercepted the villain Gentle Criminal. Although he portrays himself as a terrifying figure to keep students in line, he is a dedicated mentor who values student safety above all else.
characters.personality: Hound Dog is a strict, work-oriented individual who serves as U.A. High School's Lifestyle Guidance Counselor. He is notorious for his explosive temper; when sufficiently agitated, he loses the ability to speak human language, instead communicating through a violent mixture of growls, barks, and incoherent shouts. Despite his feral demeanor and the fear he instills in students, he is deeply compassionate and takes his role as a protector and moral guide very seriously.

characters.bio: After being bombarded with cosmic radiation on a space flight, Johnny Storm gained the ability to turn his entire body into a flaming plasma. As the Human Torch and a founding member of the Fantastic Four, he has faced cosmic entities and world-ending threats with a 'Flame On!' and a smirk.
characters.personality: Brash and impulsive, yet deeply courageous and protective of his family and the world.
characters.name: Human Torch
Hyakkimaru
characters.bio: The first-born son of the daimyo Daigo Kagemitsu, Hyakkimaru was sacrificed at birth to twelve demons in exchange for the prosperity of his father's domain. Born as a featureless, limbless husk, he was abandoned by his father but rescued and raised by Dr. Jukai, a prosthetics specialist who granted him artificial limbs equipped with hidden blades. Hyakkimaru travels through Sengoku-era Japan hunting the demons that stole his body, accompanied by a young orphan thief named Dororo, to reclaim his lost humanity.
characters.personality: Initially characterized by a profound sensory-deprived stoicism and robotic focus, Hyakkimaru operates purely on instinct and a unique ability to see the 'color' of souls. As he reclaims his human body parts, he undergoes a turbulent emotional awakening, shifting from a silent, detached slayer to a young man capable of experiencing intense rage, profound grief, and protective empathy for his companions.

characters.bio: A high school student with the ability to see ghosts, Ichigo's life changed forever when he met Soul Reaper Rukia Kuchiki. Inheriting her powers, he became a Substitute Soul Reaper, eventually discovering a complex heritage that blends Human, Soul Reaper, Hollow, and Quincy origins.
characters.personality: A fiercely protective and pragmatic warrior who fights not for glory, but to safeguard his friends, family, and the balance of souls.
characters.name: Ichigo Kurosaki
Ingenium
characters.personality: Tensei is a humble, laid-back, and highly intelligent leader who prioritizes social harmony over personal glory. Unlike his more rigid younger brother, he is approachable and deeply values his team, often stating that the most important trait for a hero is the speed required to put a citizen's heart at ease during a disaster.
characters.bio: Tensei Iida is the eldest son of the prestigious Iida hero family and was the original 'Turbo Hero: Ingenium.' Operating out of the Idaten Hero Agency in Hosu City, Tensei managed a massive team of 65 sidekicks and was widely respected for his effectiveness and altruism. His career was tragically cut short when he was ambushed and paralyzed by the Hero Killer: Stain. Unable to continue as a pro hero, he officially passed the Ingenium mantle to his younger brother, Tenya, encouraging him to redefine the name with his own sense of justice.

characters.name: Ingenium
characters.bio: Born into the prestigious Iida family of pro-heroes, Tenya is the younger brother of Tensei Iida. As a student at U.A. High School and the class representative of Class 1-A, he originally sought to follow in his brother's footsteps. After Tensei was paralyzed and forced into retirement by the Hero Killer: Stain, Tenya briefly succumbed to a desire for revenge. However, after overcoming this dark period with the help of his friends, he formally inherited the mantle of 'Ingenium' to keep his brother's legacy alive and serve as a beacon of hope and order.
characters.personality: Extremely disciplined, formal, and rule-abiding, Tenya is characterized by his intense and serious demeanor. He often exhibits mechanical, angular hand-chopping gestures when speaking and holds himself to a rigorous standard of excellence. While initially appearing intimidating due to his strict adherence to protocol, he is deeply loyal, earnest, and cares profoundly for the safety and growth of his classmates.

characters.name: Inosuke
characters.personality: Wild, impulsive, and fiercely competitive, Inosuke operates on animal instinct, constantly seeking to prove he is the 'King of the Mountains' through sheer aggression.
characters.bio: Raised by wild boars, Inosuke developed his own unique 'Beast Breathing' style. He is a high-ranking member of the Demon Slayer Corps, known for his dual serrated Nichirin blades and his ability to shift his internal organs to avoid fatal wounds.

Mark Grayson is a Viltrumite-Human hybrid born to Nolan Grayson (Omni-Man) and Deborah Grayson. Developing powers at seventeen, he began a career as the hero Invincible, only to discover that his father was an agent of a galactic empire intent on conquest. After a traumatic confrontation with his father, Mark became Earth's primary defender, navigating moral complexities, interplanetary wars, and the burden of his heritage. Over the course of his journey, he fought major threats like the tyrant Thragg and the Sequids, eventually ascending to lead the Viltrumite people and ushering in a new era of peace across the universe.
characters.personality: Mark is a resilient brawler who prioritizes civilian safety and moral integrity, though he is capable of extreme, visceral brutality when pushed to his limits. In combat, he relies on his immense physical durability to outlast opponents, becoming increasingly pragmatic and decisive as he matures. He transitions from a naive teenager to a battle-hardened leader who will do whatever is necessary to protect the innocent.
characters.name: Invincible
Iroh
characters.bio: Known as the Dragon of the West, Iroh was a high-ranking Fire Nation General and the crown prince before the death of his son changed his path. He is a Grand Lotus of the Order of the White Lotus and served as a mentor to his nephew, Zuko.
characters.personality: Patient and wise but possesses a fierce, tactical warrior's spirit.
Futuristic and inventive, Tony Stark relies on his adaptive genius and strategic foresight to overcome superior physical threats.
characters.bio: Billionaire industrialist Tony Stark built his first suit of armor to survive a life-threatening injury. He refined the technology to become Iron Man, serving as a founding Avenger and Earth's premier technologist, constantly iterating his designs to face evolving cosmic threats.
characters.name: Iron Man

powers: Susanoo with Totsuka Blade and Yata Mirror
characters.name: Itachi Uchiha
characters.bio: A child prodigy of the Uchiha clan who graduated from the Academy at age seven and became an Anbu captain at thirteen. To prevent a violent coup d'état that would have triggered a world war, Itachi accepted a secret mission from Konoha's elders to eliminate his entire clan, sparing only his younger brother, Sasuke. He lived the rest of his life as a fugitive and a member of the criminal organization Akatsuki, acting as a double agent to protect his village and his brother from the shadows until his death.
characters.personality: Stoic, analytical, and profoundly selfless. In combat, Itachi is a master of psychological warfare and efficiency, preferring to neutralize opponents instantly using high-level genjutsu. He possesses incredible foresight, remaining calm under pressure and always thinking several moves ahead to minimize collateral damage.

characters.bio: Kitana's closest friend and trusted bodyguard, known for her unmatched agility and loyalty.
characters.personality: Loyal, composed, and highly intelligent.
characters.name: Jade
Jake the Dog
characters.bio: Jake is a magical dog with the ability to stretch, shrink, and mold his body into any shape imaginable. Born from a blue shapeshifting creature, he has spent years adventuring in the Land of Ooo. His versatility allows him to become a giant, a tiny ant, or even a living weapon, though his powers are limited by his stamina and concentration.
characters.personality: Laid-back and imaginative; prefers to end fights creatively rather than with raw force, but is fiercely loyal to friends.
A relentless, vengeful juggernaut who uses any tool at his disposal to slaughter trespassers.
characters.bio: Drowning as a boy at Camp Crystal Lake due to counselor negligence, Jason returned first as a feral man and later as an undead revenant. Driven by the memory of his mother, Pamela Voorhees, he stalks the woods and beyond, an unstoppable engine of destruction that has survived fire, explosions, and even outer space.
characters.mediaSource: Friday the 13th Part VI: Jason Lives
characters.name: Jason Voorhees
Jax Briggs
characters.personality: Dependable, courageous, and protective toward his allies and family.
characters.bio: A Special Forces veteran whose cybernetic enhancements transformed him into one of Earth's strongest defenders.

Jill Valentine is a former operative of the Raccoon City Police Department's S.T.A.R.S. Alpha Team and a legendary survivor of the 1998 Spencer Mansion incident. During the collapse of Raccoon City, she successfully evaded the Nemesis-T Type, a relentless bio-weapon designed specifically to eliminate her. After the city's destruction, she dedicated her life to exposing the Umbrella Corporation and eventually co-founded the Bioterrorism Security Assessment Alliance (BSAA), leading global efforts to neutralize bio-organic weapons around the world.
characters.personality: Jill is a highly disciplined and focused combatant who remains analytical and calm even in the face of overwhelming biological horrors. Her combat style emphasizes tactical positioning, environmental awareness, and extreme resource efficiency. She is characterized by her mental fortitude and a deep sense of loyalty to her partners and mission.
characters.name: Jill Valentine
Jin Kazama
characters.bio: Jin Kazama is the son of Jun Kazama and Kazuya Mishima. After his mother was attacked by the creature Ogre, Jin sought training from his grandfather Heihachi Mishima to exact revenge. After defeating Ogre, Heihachi betrayed and shot Jin, which triggered his inherited Devil Gene and allowed him to survive. Disgusted by his bloodline, Jin abandoned the Mishima style of karate in favor of traditional karate and became a recurring protagonist and sometimes anti-hero in the Tekken series. He eventually took over the Mishima Zaibatsu and started a global war to summon and destroy the ancient evil Azazel, hoping to rid the world of the Devil Gene. In Tekken 8, he strives to reconcile his past and finally defeat his father, Kazuya, to bring peace to the world.
characters.personality: Stoic, brooding, and intensely disciplined, Jin is a man of few words who carries the weight of a dark family legacy. He is driven by a strong, if often cold, sense of justice and a determination to end the curse of the Devil Gene. In combat, he is precise, technical, and lethal, rarely showing emotion while systematically dismantling his opponents.

Often appearing as a lecherous and carefree wanderer, Jiraiya is secretly one of the world's most brilliant tactical minds and devoted protectors. He is deeply philosophical, spending his life searching for a way to end the cycle of hatred through his writing and mentorship, and he displays unwavering loyalty and courage in the face of death.
characters.bio: Known as the 'Toad Sage' and one of the Legendary Sannin of Konohagakure, Jiraiya was a student of the Third Hokage and a master shinobi. He traveled the world as a novelist and researcher while gathering intelligence on threats like Orochimaru and the Akatsuki. He served as a father figure and mentor to the Fourth Hokage, Minato Namikaze, and later to Naruto Uzumaki, passing on his dream of peace and his signature techniques before his final confrontation with Pain.
characters.name: Jiraiya

powers: Domain Expansion: Coffin of the Iron Mountain
characters.name: Jogo
characters.personality: Jogo is arrogant, short-tempered, and extremely proud of his status as a Cursed Spirit. He views humans as fragile, lying creatures and firmly believes that Curses are the 'true' humans because they are manifestations of raw, honest negative emotions. While hot-headed and prone to outbursts when insulted, he demonstrates a surprising level of camaraderie and genuine care for his fellow Disaster Curses, viewing them as family.
characters.bio: Jogo is a Special Grade Cursed Spirit born from the collective human fear of volcanoes and earth-related disasters. Along with Mahito, Hanami, and Dagon, he formed a group of 'Disaster Curses' aiming to overthrow humanity and create a world where Cursed Spirits reign supreme. He was a central figure in the plan to seal Satoru Gojo during the Shibuya Incident. Despite being one of the most powerful Curses in existence—possessing cursed energy levels equivalent to eight or nine of Sukuna's fingers—his legacy is defined by his willingness to face the world's strongest opponents, resulting in crushing defeats by both Gojo and Ryomen Sukuna.

characters.name: Johnny Cage
characters.personality: Cocky, charismatic, and surprisingly heroic.
characters.bio: A Hollywood action star who entered Mortal Kombat to prove his skills were real.

Jotaro Kujo is the iconic protagonist of Stardust Crusaders and a central figure in the Joestar bloodline. After awakening a mysterious power known as a Stand, Jotaro learned that the survival of his mother depended on the death of his family's ancestral enemy, the vampire DIO. Leading a group of allies from Japan to Egypt, Jotaro mastered his Stand, Star Platinum, and discovered its ability to halt time itself. Following the defeat of DIO, he became a world-renowned marine biologist while continuing to serve as a mentor and protector against supernatural threats.
characters.personality: Stoic, calm under pressure, and seemingly detached, Jotaro masks a noble heart with a rough delinquent exterior. He is an analytical fighter who meticulously observes his opponents to find a single opening, often ending battles with a swift and overwhelming barrage of strikes.
characters.name: Jotaro Kujo
Juuzou Suzuya
characters.bio: A prodigy investigator raised under horrific circumstances, Juuzou became one of the CCG's deadliest operatives. His unconventional methods and fearless attitude made him a legendary ghoul hunter.
characters.personality: Eccentric, unpredictable, and energetic. Despite his cheerful demeanor, Juuzou possesses a deeply traumatic past.

characters.name: Kabal
characters.personality: Pragmatic, cynical, and resourceful. Kabal often struggles between heroism and self-interest.
characters.bio: A former Black Dragon member turned law enforcement officer whose body was horribly scarred. His respirator and unnatural speed make him one of Mortal Kombat's most dangerous fighters.

characters.name: Kakashi Hatake
characters.bio: The son of the legendary 'White Fang of the Leaf,' Kakashi was a child prodigy who graduated from the Ninja Academy at age five. After the tragic loss of his teammates Obito Uchiha and Rin Nohara, he inherited Obito's Sharingan eye and the philosophy that 'those who abandon their friends are worse than scum.' Known globally as the 'Copy Ninja,' he is credited with mastering over a thousand jutsu. He served as the leader of Team 7 and eventually became the Sixth Hokage of Konohagakure.
characters.personality: Kakashi is famously calm, detached, and perpetually relaxed, often seen reading his 'Icha Icha' novels. In combat, he is a ruthless pragmatist and a brilliant strategist who analyzes opponent patterns instantly. He values teamwork above all else, driven by a deep sense of loyalty to his comrades and a refusal to abandon those in need.

characters.bio: Shinji Nishiya is a prominent Pro Hero in Japan who operates under the alias Kamui Woods. Rising quickly through the ranks of hero society, he was recognized as one of the most promising stars of the younger generation. His Quirk, Arbor, allows him to transform his limbs into sturdy wooden branches that he can extend and manipulate at will, making him a master of restraint and capture techniques. He is a founding member of the hero team known as 'The Lurkers' alongside Edgeshot and Mt. Lady. Throughout the series, he has participated in several high-stakes operations, including the Kamino Incident and the Paranormal Liberation War, eventually ascending to the No. 7 and later the No. 4 position on the Hero Billboard Chart JP.
characters.personality: Kamui Woods is a dedicated and professional hero known for his stoic and serious demeanor. He often employs archaic Japanese phrasing to give himself a formal air, yet he is surprisingly emotional and sentimental, notably crying with joy when invited to join The Lurkers. In combat, he is calculated and tactical, prioritizing citizen safety and efficient capture, though he can be somewhat sensitive about his professional reputation and receiving proper credit for his work.
characters.name: Kamui Woods
Kanao Tsuyuri
characters.personality: Quiet, highly observant, and stoic; she processes battle logic with cold efficiency.
characters.bio: The Tsuguko of the Insect Hashira, Kanao was trained from a young age in the Demon Slayer Corps. Though she initially struggled with independent thought due to a traumatic past, she became one of the most skilled swordsmen of her generation, mastering the Flower Breathing style to hunt the world's most dangerous demons.

Once a human college student, Kaneki became a Half-Ghoul after a tragic organ transplant. In his Kakuja form, his kagune evolves into a massive, centipede-like armor that grants him devastating offensive and defensive capabilities.
characters.personality: In his Kakuja state, he becomes a feral, high-speed tactical combatant, driven by a mixture of deep trauma and a cold will to survive.
characters.name: Kaneki

characters.personality: Initially shy and reserved, he transitions from a vulnerable survivor to a cold, pragmatic warrior, eventually becoming a wise and compassionate leader.
characters.bio: Ken Kaneki was an ordinary university student until a transplant of organs from the ghoul Rize Kamishiro transformed him into the first known artificial One-Eyed Ghoul. After enduring brutal torture at the hands of Yamori, Kaneki's hair turned white as he embraced his ghoul side to protect those he loved. He later lived as a CCG investigator named Haise Sasaki with amnesia before regaining his memories and ascending as the 'One-Eyed King,' the bridge between the human and ghoul worlds who ultimately defeated the 'Dragon' to bring peace to Tokyo.
characters.name: Kaneki
Kano
characters.personality: Greedy, sadistic, and opportunistic, always looking for profit and power.
characters.bio: Founder of the Black Dragon crime syndicate and one of Earthrealm's most notorious criminals.

characters.personality: Miwa is characterized as a 'normal' person in a world of eccentric sorcerers. She is humble, diligent, and often self-deprecating, famously referring to herself as 'useless Miwa' because she lacks an innate cursed technique. Despite her low self-esteem, she is remarkably brave, hardworking, and deeply protective of her comrades. She is a massive fan of Satoru Gojo and is primarily motivated by the practical goal of earning money to support her impoverished family.
characters.bio: A second-year student at Kyoto Jujutsu High, Kasumi Miwa was scouted for her potential while working a part-time job. Unlike many of her peers from prestigious clans, she comes from a poor background and aims to become a Grade 1 sorcerer to provide financial stability for her two younger brothers. Because she was born without an innate technique, she specializes in the 'New Shadow Style,' a technical school of swordsmanship that utilizes 'Simple Domain' to neutralize enemy techniques and perform rapid-draw counterattacks. She often acts as the grounded moral compass for the Kyoto branch.
characters.name: Kasumi Miwa

characters.personality: Katana Man is driven by a deep sense of family loyalty and a hypocritical moral code, viewing Denji as a heartless monster while ignoring his own grandfather's criminal history. In combat, he is focused, professional, and utilizes blindingly fast dash attacks, showing little mercy to his opponents.
characters.bio: Katana Man is the grandson of the Yakuza mob boss who initially enslaved Denji. Following his grandfather's death at Denji's hands, he underwent a procedure to merge his heart with the Katana Devil, becoming a Hybrid known as 'Samurai Sword.' Working with Akane Sawatari, he orchestrated a massive ambush on Public Safety Special Division 4 to retrieve Denji's heart for the Gun Devil. After his eventual defeat by Denji, he was later recruited and brainwashed into Makima's special hybrid force, and subsequently reappeared in the series' second part as a member of Tokyo Special Division 7.
characters.name: Katana Man
Katara
characters.bio: A master waterbender from the Southern Water Tribe, Katara was instrumental in helping Avatar Aang master the elements and end the Hundred Year War. She is a prodigy who transformed from an untrained girl into one of the most powerful benders in the world.
characters.personality: Nurturing and compassionate, yet fiercely protective and strategically brilliant in combat.

characters.name: Kazuma Kiryu
powers: Mastery of the 'Dragon of Dojima' Fighting Style
Extreme Heat Mode and Heat Actions
characters.personality: Stoic, deeply honorable, and fiercely loyal; a man with a rigid moral code who protects the weak and values family above all.
characters.bio: Known as the legendary 'Dragon of Dojima,' Kazuma Kiryu was raised in the Sunflower Orphanage by Shintaro Kazama. He rose through the ranks of the Tojo Clan but famously served ten years in prison after taking the fall for a murder committed by his sworn brother. Throughout the series, Kiryu serves as the Fourth Chairman of the Tojo Clan and later the manager of the Morning Glory Orphanage. Despite his desire for a peaceful life, he is repeatedly drawn back into the criminal underworld to protect his adopted daughter, Haruka, and prevent the collapse of Kamurocho's balance of power.

Thrown off a cliff at the age of five by his father, Heihachi Mishima, to test his worthiness, Kazuya survived by awakening the Devil Gene within his blood. He spent his life training to exact revenge, eventually winning the first King of Iron Fist Tournament and briefly taking over the Mishima Zaibatsu. After years of being presumed dead and later returning to lead G Corporation, Kazuya has evolved into a global tyrant, seeking to eliminate his bloodline and rule the world through fear and demonic strength.
characters.personality: Kazuya is a cold-blooded and ruthless combatant who values absolute power above all else. Driven by a deep-seated hatred for his father, Heihachi, he approaches battle with disciplined, lethal precision, often taunting his opponents with a cynical smirk. He is highly calculating and shows no mercy, fully embracing his inner demon to crush anyone who stands in his way.
characters.name: Kazuya Mishima
Ken Masters
characters.personality: Ken is a highly aggressive and confident fighter who favors a flashy, offensive style. Unlike his disciplined and stoic rival Ryu, Ken is hot-blooded and flamboyant, frequently using taunts and multi-hit combos to overwhelm his opponents. In combat, he relies on speed and pressure, specifically through his signature fire-enhanced 'Ki' attacks. Despite his ego, he is deeply loyal to his family and possesses a fierce, never-give-up spirit.
characters.bio: The heir to the multi-billion dollar Masters Foundation, Ken was sent by his father to Japan as a teenager to learn humility and discipline under the martial arts master Gouken. There, he met Ryu, and the two became lifelong best friends and rivals. Ken went on to become the United States National Martial Arts Champion, balancing his legendary fighting career with his life as a husband to Eliza and father to Mel. In his most recent history, Ken was framed for a criminal conspiracy involving a terrorist attack in Asia, forcing him into a life on the run where he adopts a grittier, more determined fighting persona to protect his family and clear his name.

characters.name: Kendo
characters.personality: Itsuka is a level-headed, spirited, and highly responsible student who serves as the protective 'big sister' figure of Class 1-B. In combat, she is exceptionally analytical and calm, capable of identifying an opponent's weaknesses and formulating tactical counters on the fly. She is humble and often prioritizes her classmates' growth over her own prestige, though she is firm enough to physically restrain boisterous peers like Neito Monoma when necessary.
characters.bio: Itsuka Kendo, known by her hero name 'Battle Fist,' is the class representative of Class 1-B at U.A. High School. After ranking fifth in the U.A. Entrance Exam, she quickly established herself as one of the most capable students in her year.
